Docs.summary: Provides generic value types for representing colors in the ARGB and RGBA color spaces, along with helper methods for creating and converting color values with explicit endianness.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
The <xref:System.Numerics.Colors> namespace contains generic color structs that store alpha, red, green, and blue components in a specified order. The two primary types are:
- <xref:System.Numerics.Colors.Argb`1> — a readonly struct that stores color components in alpha, red, green, blue order. - <xref:System.Numerics.Colors.Rgba`1> — a readonly struct that stores color components in red, green, blue, alpha order.
Both structs are generic over the component type `T` (constrained to value types), which lets you use `byte`, `float`, or other numeric types for the color components. Each struct implements <xref:System.IEquatable`1> and provides methods for copying components to a span and converting between ARGB and RGBA representations.
The <xref:System.Numerics.Colors.Argb> and <xref:System.Numerics.Colors.Rgba> static classes provide factory methods for creating color instances from `uint` values with explicit big-endian or little-endian byte ordering, and extension methods for converting color instances back to `uint` values.
